options: Error — you can't pass multiple strings to print()
challenges.explanation: When you separate items with commas inside print(), Python automatically adds a single space between each one. So the three strings are joined with spaces, giving you: Hello World from Python!

challenges.prompt: This code has a bug. What is wrong?
options: print() cannot take more than one item
The text Hello and World! are missing quotes — Python needs quotes around text to treat it as a string
There should be a semicolon at the end of the line
challenges.explanation: Without quotes, Python tries to look up Hello and World! as variable names rather than treating them as text. This causes a SyntaxError. The fix is: print("Hello, World!") — always wrap your text in quotes.

challenges.prompt: Complete the code so it prints: Learning Python is fun
challenges.explanation: print() is the built-in Python command that displays text on the screen. You put the message inside its parentheses, wrapped in quotes.

challenges.prompt: Put these lines in the right order so the program prints three lines: Hello!, I am learning Python., See you soon!
challenges.explanation: Each print() call outputs on its own line and runs from top to bottom. So the first print() gives Hello!, then I am learning Python., and finally See you soon!
